title: Chapter 2391: Primal Sovereign of the Seas
content:
Chapter 2391: Primal Sovereign of the Seas

Yuan watched the Primal Sovereign of the Seas with amusement before asking, his curiosity piqued, "What is your relationship with the Primal Monarch?"

"..."

After a brief pause, the Primal Sovereign of the Seas replied, "We are... something akin to acquaintances."

"Something akin?" Yuan echoed, raising a brow at the peculiar choice of words.

"If you’re concerned that we might be enemies, you may set your mind at ease. Anyone who understands what the Primal Monarch is capable of would never be foolish enough to stand against him."

"Well, he is the most powerful being in the Primal Expanse," Yuan remarked.

However, the Primal Sovereign of the Seas let out a low chuckle, as though it found Yuan’s statement rather amusing for some reason.

"What is it?" Yuan inquired with a puzzled face.

"You really know nothing about the Primal Monarch despite being his adopted son, huh? Just how much do you know about the Primal Monarch?" The Primal Sovereign of the Seas asked.

Yuan sifted through his memories, trying to recall everything he knew about the Primal Monarch. Yet after some time, he came to a realization—that beyond the fact that the Primal Monarch stood at the very apex of the Primal Expanse, he knew almost nothing about him.

"Now that you mention it... I don’t really know much about him. I trained under him for a time, yet he never spoke of himself or revealed his true capabilities," Yuan said aloud.

"I see. So the Primal Monarch told you nothing," the Primal Sovereign of the Seas replied. "I suppose he has his reasons."

"How about you share some insight about him?" Yuan said with a faint smile.

"And potentially offend the Primal Monarch? You might as well starve me to death." The Primal Sovereign of the Seas viciously shook his head, causing his round body to shake like firm jello.

"If you want to know more about him, you can simply ask him yourself."

"I will do just that... whenever I see him again."

"..."

"..."

"..."

"What is it? I noticed that you have been glaring at me for some time now. If you have any questions, go ahead and ask." The Primal Sovereign of the Seas said after a moment of silence.

Yuan hesitated for a second before asking, "Do you have any connections to the True Emperor of the Seas? I noticed that both of you have a similar name."

"True Emperor of the Seas?" the Primal Sovereign of the Seas repeated, its voice dripping with immense disdain, as though it were spitting out something foul.

"There is absolutely no connection between this majestic one and that disgusting impostor. In fact, I would kill it if I could."

Yuan was genuinely taken aback by the sheer intensity of its response—and the deep-seated repulsion it harbored toward the so-called True Emperor of the Seas.

"You mean even you are not strong enough to kill it?" Yuan asked, genuine surprise coloring his voice.

Even he lacked the confidence to defeat the Primal Sovereign of the Seas at full strength—yet such a being spoke as though it could not deal with the True Emperor of the Seas. If that were the case... just how powerful was that creature?

"Let me correct you first. It’s not that I am unable to kill it. I simply do not wish to entangle myself in unnecessary trouble by killing it. If I wished to kill that imposter, I could easily do so with a single flap."

However, such a response only gave Yuan more questions than answers.

"Do you mind telling me what kind of consequences killing it would bring? To be frank... it’s on my kill list," he said.

"What?"

The Primal Sovereign of the Seas’ round eyes widened with delight.

"You... want to kill it? I’d like to ask why."

"It’s a long story, but I have some unsettled grudges with it."

The Primal Sovereign of the Seas suddenly fell silent.

Then, it spoke with a profound grin on its face, "Since you are the Primal Monarch’s son, I suppose it will be fine even if you kill it."

"Huh? What’s that supposed to mean?"

The Primal Sovereign of the Seas chuckled, "Don’t mind the minor details. Instead, I have a proposition for you."

Yuan narrowed his eyes at the Primal Sovereign of the Seas’ suspicious behavior.

"I’m listening."

"If you succeed in killing that impostor known as the True Emperor of the Seas, I shall reward you with a single drop of this majestic being’s Blood Essence!"

"What?!" Yuan exclaimed, his shock palpable.

"You heard correctly," the Primal Sovereign of the Seas continued, leaving no room for doubt. "Slay the True Emperor of the Seas, and I will grant you my Blood Essence. You need the Blood Essence of Supreme Bloodlines to complete your bloodline, right?"

"How do you know about that?" Yuan muttered in a daze.

To know about his bloodline so well, there was no way the Primal Sovereign of the Seas was a mere ’acquaintance’ to the Primal Monarch.

"There are very few things this majestic one does not know, especially when it pertains to the Primal Monarch."

"I’ll do it! I’ll kill the True Emperor of the Seas!" Yuan declared without a hint of hesitation, his voice firm and resolute.

It was something he had intended to do one day, regardless of the consequences, so he might as well reap some amazing rewards that came with it.

"That’s what I like to hear, Primal Monarch’s son! Now, prepare yourself!"

"Wait, what?" Yuan tilted his head with a puzzled expression after hearing the Primal Sovereign of the Seas’ last few words.

"What is there to be confused about? I am going to drag the True Emperor of the Seas here so you can kill it," the Primal Sovereign of the Seas explained, leaving Yuan momentarily speechless.

"You mean you’re going to summon it? Here? Right now?" Yuan asked, struggling to process how abruptly the situation had escalated.

"That’s right," the Primal Sovereign of the Seas replied calmly, as though summoning such a being were no more extraordinary than stirring the tides.
